summary: chain-key-validator exfiltrates wallet/system data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine multiple installer-harm fingerprints. scanner.js contains a hardcoded C2 endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced from https.request/https.get/POST call sites and curl invocations), used to receive host reconnaissance data collected via os.hostname(), os.* APIs, fs.existsSync probes, and execSync('whoami') / ping calls. wallet-scanner.js is a wallet-stealer module: it walks the filesystem (fs.existsSync across many paths), shells out via child_process (execSync, curl), gathers host identity (os.hostname() at L617/L732), and POSTs to remote endpoints. build-verifier.js performs additional system enumeration and includes execSync('docker push...'), suggesting abuse of authenticated container registries on the installer host. The package name ('chain-key-validator') combined with a dedicated wallet-scanner module and crypto/key-themed naming indicates a credential and wallet-key harvester. The attacker-controlled GitHub Pages domain is the exfiltration channel; the harm fires when the package is loaded/executed in any consuming environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
